title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Ownership, Access Points, and Public Access · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds least-privilege S3 authorization proof, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of least-privilege S3 authorization proof while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Principal Action Resource Condition
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 2457 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Principal Action Resource Condition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Principal Action Resource Condition, keep Principal Action Resource Condition connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
